title: Contains Duplicate
description: Given an integer array `nums`, return `true` if any value appears at least twice in the array, and return `false` if every element is distinct.
inputFormat: An integer array nums.
outputFormat: Boolean true if duplicate exists, false otherwise.
hints: Try using a hash set to track visited numbers.
Scan array once. Set lookups are O(1) time.

editorial.solutionOverview: Maintain a single-pass scan. Keep track of visited numbers using a HashSet. If we meet a number that is already in the set, we return true.

approaches.title: HashSet Lookups
approaches.description: Single-pass scan using a HashSet. Constant time lookups.

title: Valid Anagram
description: Given two strings `s` and `t`, return `true` if `t` is an anagram of `s`, and `false` otherwise.
inputFormat: Two strings s and t.
outputFormat: Boolean true if anagram, false otherwise.
hints: Count frequency of each character.
If both have equal character frequency tables, they are anagrams.

editorial.solutionOverview: Maintain a character counter array of size 26 (assuming English lowercase letters). Loop s and increment frequencies. Loop t and decrement. If all frequencies are zero, return true.

approaches.title: Frequency Counter Array
approaches.description: Single array mapping counts for 26 alphabets.

title: Two Sum
description:
Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to `target`.

You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.
inputFormat: An integer array nums, and integer target.
outputFormat: Array of two indexes [i, j].
hints: Can we use a hash map to search for the complement (target - nums[i]) in constant time?
Map values to their index during scanning.

editorial.solutionOverview: Maintain a hash map. For each number, calculate its complement (target - nums[i]). If complement is in map, return indices immediately. Otherwise, add current number to map.

approaches.title: HashMap Complement Search
approaches.description: Single pass lookup using a HashMap.

title: Product of Array Except Self
description: Given an integer array `nums`, return an array `answer` such that `answer[i]` is equal to the product of all the elements of `nums` except `nums[i]`. Must run in O(N) time and without using the division operator.
inputFormat: An integer array nums.
outputFormat: An integer array answer.
hints: Compute prefix products in a left-to-right pass.
Compute suffix products in a right-to-left pass and multiply.

editorial.solutionOverview: Maintain prefix product dynamically inside the result array. Then loop backward and accumulate the suffix product while multiplying it into each index.

approaches.title: Prefix and Suffix Accumulation
approaches.description: Point-wise multiplication of left prefixes and right suffixes.

title: Maximum Subarray (Kadane)
description: Given an integer array `nums`, find the subarray with the largest sum and return its sum.
inputFormat: An integer array nums.
outputFormat: Max sum computed.
hints: At each element, choose whether to extend the existing subarray or start a fresh one.
Keep track of global max sum.

editorial.solutionOverview: Apply Kadanes Algorithm: maintain a running currentSum. If currentSum drops below 0, reset it to 0. At each step, update global max.

approaches.title: Kadanes Linear Scan
approaches.description: Single pass Kadanes algorithm.

title: Advanced Challenge #6: Optimize ARRAYS Bounds
description: Given an optimized ARRAYS setup, write an efficient, high-performance function to index, rearrange, or process element arrays matching elite coding assessments.
inputFormat: A structured array representing ARRAYS nodes.
outputFormat: The optimal resulting complexity computed matching strict boundaries.
hints: Analyze edge cases first (e.g. empty inputs, single element lists).
Use double pointers or memoization arrays to minimize loops.

editorial.solutionOverview: To solve this advanced ARRAYS question, we scan the input once and apply the prefix index technique. This resolves computations in linear time.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.description: Single scan pass tracking pointers to eliminate nested operations.
